title: How to Ace Your Computer Science Exam
excerpt: Five proven study strategies every CS student should know — from active recall to spaced repetition.
content:
Start with the Fundamentals

Before diving into complex topics, make sure you have a rock-solid understanding of the basics. Number systems, Boolean logic, and basic programming constructs are tested in almost every exam.

Use Active Recall

Instead of re-reading notes, close your book and try to recall key concepts from memory. Research shows this is far more effective than passive revision. The flashcards section of this site is perfect for this technique.

Practice Past Papers

Past exam papers are gold. Time yourself strictly and mark your own work against the mark scheme. Pay attention to command words — "state", "describe", and "explain" all require different depths of answer.

Teach What You Know

Explaining a concept to someone else (or even to a rubber duck) is one of the best ways to consolidate understanding. If you cannot explain it simply, you do not understand it well enough yet.

Space Your Revision

Cramming the night before is far less effective than spaced repetition over several weeks. Revisit topics at increasing intervals — after 1 day, 3 days, 1 week, then 2 weeks.
author: CS Teacher
